evidence_quotes.quote: dark patterns that seek to deceive the user

paragraphs.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. The initiative carried out by the EU seems to me to be a project that makes it possible to give due weight to a problem that is increasingly present in our society and is very unnoticed for the average consumer: the protection of consumers and users in digital matters. When shopping in online shops, visits to websites, online games, our information (although it does not appear that we do not perceive it as if it can be when signing a physical contract) is very unprotected and clicked on a large number of tabs, where we are asked to accept the cookies on that platform, losing much of our data protection without knowing us. For the sake of completeness, there are also so-called dark patterns that seek to deceive the user, such as insistence, targeted advertising, infinite scrolling all of this, without practically knowing about it, causes manipulation in a very veiled way, as they are protected after a screen and, moreover, are patterns that unfortunately have very standardised standards. I therefore believe that more information is needed in this area. The future is artificial intelligence, technology and the digital world, so it is of great importance that, just as we have ensured legal certainty when it comes to making physical contracts and maintaining social relationships as a mere buying or buying a mobile phone in a shop, for example; we need to adapt to the new times and ensure these same measures, but in the digital world. If the future is digitalisation, we must live in a secure digitalised world, where any consumer has the same tools to understand and defend themselves against the dangers that a simple tab or visit a certain website may entail.
